g.text: StatBus Upgrade Timeline (single service; ground truth decides direction — forward when at-target, identity-keyed restore only when positively behind)
Operator / CI
Operator / CI
systemd (user)
systemd (user)
Upgrade Service
statbus-upgrade@$USER
Upgrade Service
statbus-upgrade@$USER
PostgreSQL
public.upgrade + snapshot
PostgreSQL
public.upgrade + snapshot
Fresh install — seed restore (first boot, before any upgrade)
./sb install (StateFresh) — Seed step:
./sb db seed fetch (pull statbus-seed:<commit_short>) + pg_restore --single-transaction
./sb migrate up — apply only the migrations newer than the seed
Fresh DB only: checkSeedRestored gates the Seed step OFF when the DB holds
user data or applied migrations — a seed restore never overwrites a populated DB.
Seed CREATION is a CI/dev activity — see DEVELOPMENT.md § Database seed.
Service boot
start statbus-upgrade@$USER.service (Type=notify, Restart=always)
advisory lock + LISTEN upgrade channel
(cheap, bounded init)
sd_notify READY=1
(emitted here — heavy recovery below runs in the ACTIVE phase under WatchdogSec)
boot migrate up — bring schema to HEAD (schema-skew guard)
recoverFromFlag — reconcile a flag left on disk (flag-DRIVEN)
completeInProgressUpgrade — reconcile an in_progress row with NO flag
(flag-INDEPENDENT belt; the SOLE recovery for a pre-flight kill)
Two-layer boot recovery. recoverFromFlag handles a kill AFTER the flag exists (Phase
ladder: PreSwap / PostSwap / Resuming). completeInProgressUpgrade handles a kill BEFORE
the flag exists — the claim -> writeUpgradeFlag window (see § Execute upgrade (pre-swap)).
It rolls such an orphan to a named terminal state via verifyUpgradeGroundTruth.
TEST COVERAGE — boot
TEST 1-boot-startup-timeout: startup past TimeoutStartSec stays bounded —
heavy work runs post-READY=1 under WatchdogSec, never the static start budget.
TEST 1-boot-advisory-too-early: an advisory-lock attempt before the DB is ready
exits 42 and systemd restarts cleanly — no start-loop wedge.
NO TEST (gap): the belt rolling a flagless in_progress row to terminal is unproven —
see the preflight-kill gap under § Execute upgrade (pre-swap).
Schedule + dispatch
./sb upgrade apply-latest →
UPDATE state='scheduled' + NOTIFY
Deploy contract (STATBUS-040/-041): deploy scripts
(standalone.sh / cloud.sh) NEVER pre-stop the service —
systemctl stop is SIGTERM, which an in-flight upgrade
answers with a rollback. ./sb install itself refuses a
progressing upgrade and takes over a crash-looping one
(SIGKILL-class). Binary swaps are atomic renames — no
stop needed for those either.
NOTIFY wakes the listener
claim row WHERE state='scheduled' → in_progress
(started_at set, error=NULL — NO flag on disk yet)
+ reset-failed on own unit (NRestarts counts THIS upgrade only — STATBUS-039 F2)
Execute upgrade (pre-swap)
pre-flight — NO flag on disk yet (row is in_progress, error=NULL)
pre-flight checks — downgrade guard, release assets, disk, signature
Flag-absent window — spans the claim above through pre-flight, until writeUpgradeFlag.
A kill HERE leaves the row in_progress | error=NULL with NO flag: recoverFromFlag finds
nothing to reconcile, so completeInProgressUpgrade (§ Service boot) is the ONLY recovery
— it rolls the row back with a named reason at the next boot.
NO TEST (gap): preflight-kill — a kill in the claim -> writeUpgradeFlag window must converge
the row to a named terminal state via the belt at next boot. Candidate scenario:
killed-by-system-during-preflight-before-flag.
mutex held — Holder="service", flock LOCK_EX on the marker fd
writeUpgradeFlag — acquireFlock opens the marker (Phase=PreSwap)
maintenance mode on
take DB snapshot -> backup_path
git checkout target commit
replaceBinaryOnDisk -> stamp Phase=PostSwap
Fail-fast: any step (pre- or post-swap) that errors OR hangs aborts the
single attempt and routes to rollback (§ Complete / rollback). No retry.
TEST COVERAGE — pre-swap (mutex held)
TEST 2-preswap-backup-kill: a kill during the DB snapshot converges the row to a named
terminal — rolled_back, or failed if the restore also fails — never stranded in_progress.
TEST 2-preswap-checkout-kill: a kill during git checkout converges to terminal; the
unswapped binary keeps serving the old version.
TEST 2-preswap-binary-swap-kill: a kill at the replaceBinaryOnDisk pivot is recovered (PostSwap
resume or rollback) — never a half-swapped row that lies about the running version.
Binary-swap restart + resume (post-swap, on the NEW binary)
exit 42 — self-update, request the ONE planned respawn
restart unit on the new binary
fresh boot: cheap init -> READY=1 (watchdog arms)
boot-migrate-up (:1644) — applies the ENTIRE pending migration delta
The new binary BOOTS FRESH — nothing continues in memory; it re-runs the full Service-boot
sequence: cheap init -> READY=1 -> boot-migrate-up (:1644) -> recoverFromFlag (:1689).
Because the pre-swap checkout already put the repo at the NEW version, boot-migrate-up is
the step that ACTUALLY APPLIES every upgrade's migrations — the applyPostSwap migrate below
is a no-op in the normal flow. boot-migrate runs post-READY with the watchdog armed and no
other WATCHDOG=1 source alive, so it carries its OWN always-ping ticker, bounded by the
shared 30-min MigrateUpTimeout (STATBUS-012; same exemption as the applyPostSwap migrate).
recoverFromFlag (service.go:682) then READS the flag's persisted Phase to learn where the
prior process died. Phase progresses PreSwap "" (:197) -> PostSwap "post_swap" (stamped
:403, before exit 42) -> Resuming (stamped :4339, as resume commits). The SAME dispatch
runs on EVERY boot — the planned exit-42 here, or any unplanned-death restart.
alt
[flag.Phase = PostSwap — the PLANNED exit-42 handoff (the ONE legitimate continue)]
resumePostSwap (:774) -> stamp Phase=Resuming (:4339)
applyPostSwap — remaining steps run on the new binary
config generate + docker compose pull
start DB + wait health + reconnect
./sb migrate up (no-op normally — boot-migrate above did the work;
bounded retry executor on the STATBUS-017 fall-through)
start app services + health check
maintenance off + archive backup
[flag.Phase = PreSwap — died BEFORE the binary swap]
recoveryRollback -> roll back (§ Complete / rollback)
restore no-ops BY IDENTITY: PreSwap carries an empty BackupPath
[flag.Phase = Resuming — died DURING the resume: GROUND TRUTH decides (STATBUS-039)]
alt
[at-or-past target, or unverifiable (DB unreachable)]
resume FORWARD again — never restore an at-target box
(each retry loud; failure stamped non-terminally on the row)
[positively behind (binary mismatch / migrations missing, DB reachable)]
recoveryRollback -> one-shot rollback to THIS upgrade's
OWN snapshot (flag.BackupPath, identity-keyed; flock-serialized)
recoverFromFlag is a PHASE dispatch with ground truth deciding direction BEFORE anything
destructive (STATBUS-039: forward when logically possible — a died attempt is not
impossibility). PostSwap continues; PreSwap rolls back (nothing to restore by identity);
Resuming consults verifyUpgradeGroundTruthEx — AtTarget/Unknown resume forward, only a
POSITIVELY-BEHIND verdict restores. The binary check (verifyBinaryGroundTruth) is
tri-state: merge-base exit 1 = the only Behind; any git error (shallow clone, exit 128)
Unknown -> forward, never a restore on clone-state evidence. An unknown Phase value
fails loud (FLAG_PHASE_UNKNOWN).
TEST COVERAGE — phase dispatch + post-swap resume
TEST 3-postswap-resume-died-rollback — ON HOLD, EXPECTATION OBSOLETE (STATBUS-039/-044):
the pre-039 assertion (death during Resuming => always rolled_back, bounded NRestarts)
no longer holds. Post-039 behavior is a four-case verdict matrix: (0) at-target +
containers already at flag target => immediate canary self-heal => completed (the rune
shape — covered by the rune-wedge scenario); (1) at-target + TRANSIENT failure =>
forward retry succeeds => completed; (2) at-target + PERSISTENT failure (this
scenario's pinned-kill shape) => loops forward BY DESIGN, row stays in_progress +
loud, NO rollback — what to assert awaits the King's loudness decision (observability
escalation after N forward failures vs loop-loud-forever; direction never changes);
(3) positively-behind => one-shot rollback to the upgrade's OWN snapshot
(UPGRADE_DIED_DURING_RESUME). Rewrite per STATBUS-044 AFTER loudness is settled.
GREEN: 3-postswap-mid-migration-kill / 3-postswap-mid-tx-kill / 3-postswap-between-migrations-kill /
3-postswap-migrate-killed-after-commit / 3-postswap-migration-deterministic-error (the last two
GREEN since the STATBUS-017 fix landed — see cells (2)/(e)).
The cells of the commit<->record boundary, in execution order:
(1) mid-migration-kill: BEFORE the migration's tx opens (db.migration max unchanged, no schema
change) — forward re-applies cleanly -> completed. GREEN.
(b) mid-tx-kill: kill INSIDE the migration's tx (after BEGIN, before COMMIT) -> Postgres aborts
the uncommitted tx (no committed-but-unrecorded state) -> forward re-applies cleanly ->
completed. GREEN. (3-postswap-mid-tx-kill — the green control proving the boundary's SAFE
side, vs the after-commit cells (2)/(e).)
(2) migrate-killed-after-commit: in the ~ms window AFTER commit, BEFORE the db.migration INSERT
(db.migration max unchanged, but the schema object is already committed). Forward re-apply
FAILS ("relation already exists"), so the ONLY coherent recovery is RESTORE -> rolled_back
(the rune shape).
BEHAVIOR (STATBUS-017 FIXED, routing updated by STATBUS-039): the schema-skew-guard
`./sb migrate up` runs BEFORE recoverFromFlag on BOTH recovery entrypoints (service boot;
./sb install crash recovery) and re-runs the committed-unrecorded migration -> "relation
already exists" -> migrate up FAILS. With a service-held in-progress flag present the guard
FALLS THROUGH to recoverFromFlag instead of markTerminal+return. Post-039 the Resuming
branch consults ground truth: db.migration max < on-disk max with a reachable DB =
POSITIVELY BEHIND -> recoveryRollback -> identity-keyed snapshot restore -> rolled_back.
Same terminal as pre-039, now selected by verdict rather than an unconditional latch.
(The refuse path is kept for the no-flag / install-held case. The dead
forward-recovery+restore segment that used to follow the phase branches was DELETED
outright in STATBUS-039 — unknown phases now fail loud.)
3-postswap-migrate-killed-after-commit is the GREEN regression reproducer: it FABRICATES this
state deterministically (committed-but-unrecorded migration object + R1 pinned pre-upgrade
branch + R2 seeded snapshot + R3 tracked migration commit) and asserts rolled_back.
(3) between-migrations-kill: AFTER N is recorded, BEFORE N+1 opens (db.migration max +1) —
resumes the unrecorded pending set N+1..HEAD cleanly -> completed. GREEN.
(e) migration-deterministic-error (3-postswap-migration-deterministic-error): a migration whose
apply errors EVERY time -> migrate up fails the same way as (2); with a service-held flag the
guard FALLS THROUGH to recoverFromFlag -> Resuming branch -> ground truth POSITIVELY BEHIND
(migrations missing, DB reachable) -> identity-keyed restore -> rolled_back. Forward is
impossible (errors every apply), so restore is the only escape — the sharpest case for the
fix, and exactly the case ground truth selects backward for. GREEN since STATBUS-017.
The commit<->record cells above all fire at the BOOT-MIGRATE step (the pre-recoverFromFlag
schema-skew guard, :1644 / install_upgrade.go:198) — that is where the migrations run on
recovery boots, not at the applyPostSwap migrate.
TEST 3-postswap-migration-timeout (service-dispatch rewrite, 908191f0c): a >WatchdogSec
migration at the post-swap boot-migrate must NOT be watchdog-killed — RED until the
STATBUS-012 always-ping ticker lands, GREEN after (the suite's watchdog/Race-B net; the
pre-rewrite inline version was vacuous — no systemd in its flow).
DEFERRED (not in CI): 3-postswap-worker-ddl-deadlock — a worker-locked migration stays
bounded via pre-DDL quiesce; listed here as intent, not as proven coverage.
TEST 3-postswap-container-restart-kill: a kill between step 11 (start app services) and step 12
(health) is recovered by the next install re-running 11+12 idempotently -> completed.
TEST 3-postswap-watchdog-reconnect: a slow-but-advancing DB reconnect is kept alive by the
deferGating WATCHDOG=1 ticker — systemd does NOT falsely reap it; a true >5-min hang is reaped
by connect()'s own 5-min ctx (via d.reconnect()), not the watchdog.
TEST 3-postswap-archivebackup-watchdog: on the SCHEDULED path (already active-phase) the ticker
SCOPE covers archiveBackup — a multi-minute tar is not falsely reaped.
TEST 3-postswap-archivebackup-resume: on the exit-42 RESUME path, READY=1-before-recoverFromFlag
(plan piece #2) puts the resume in the ACTIVE phase, and the terminal state='completed' UPDATE
persists BEFORE the non-critical tar (FIX A) — a slow tar can never strand the row in_progress
(the NO/rune 40 h wedge).
Complete / rollback
alt
[all steps succeeded]
mark completed_at (state=completed)
removeUpgradeFlag (mutex released)
supersede older rows + notify UI
Slack callback — OK
TEST 0-happy-upgrade: an injection-free scheduled upgrade runs claim -> pre-swap -> exit-42 ->
applyPostSwap -> this success branch (state=completed, flag removed) — the baseline the
post-swap failure scenarios perturb.
[step failed/hung AND ground truth POSITIVELY BEHIND (STATBUS-039 —
at-target/unverifiable failures never reach here: they stamp the row's error
non-terminally and retry FORWARD on the next recovery pass)]
rollback() — restore git + restore DB snapshot
(identity-keyed: ONLY this upgrade's recorded snapshot; missing => failed,
never another backup; recovery entry is flock-serialized at recoveryRollback)
restart services (docker compose up)
wait for DB health (30s)
SYMMETRIC with applyPostSwap (service.go:3780) — must NOT reconnect before the DB is ready (the scenario-21 race)
reconnect (re-acquire authority lock) + write terminal state (bounded retry)
alt
[snapshot restored cleanly AND services healthy]
state=rolled_back (+ error) — healthy at old version
[restore also failed]
state=failed (+ error) — needs hands-on
removeUpgradeFlag — ONLY on a landed write
(else KEEP: dead-PID breadcrumb -> StateCrashedUpgrade / belt reconciles)
Slack callback — FAILED siren (fires regardless of the write)
TEST COVERAGE — complete / rollback
TEST 4-rollback-kill: a kill mid-rollback() still converges to a terminal state
on the next recovery pass.
GAP -> FIX (surfaced RED by 2-preswap-backup-kill):
ROOT: rollback() restarted the DB then reconnected with NO DB-health-wait — asymmetric with
applyPostSwap's waitForDBHealth(30s) (service.go:3780) — so the reconnect raced the DB
restart and transiently failed. The terminal UPDATE was then single-shot, silently swallowed,
and the flag removed unconditionally -> row stranded in_progress. The retry alone would only
have papered over the missing wait.
FIX (1) add waitForDBHealth(30s) after restart, before reconnect — the root fix, symmetric with
the normal path (drawn above). (2) the terminal write retries the FULL reconnect (re-acquiring
the upgrade_daemon AUTHORITY lock, service.go:2660 / 1922) + markTerminal on exhaustion +
removeUpgradeFlag ONLY on a landed write (KEEP on failure). On lock-contention, YIELD to the
authority — the belt reconciles; never bypass the lock.
